This matches the prior behavior in server.ts.\\n */\\nexport async function registerAllRoutes(deps: RouteRegistryDeps, hooks: RouteRegistrationHooks = {}): Promise<RouteRegistrationReport> {\\n  const manifest = buildRouteManifest(deps);\\n  const report: RouteRegistrationReport = { loaded: [], skipped: [], attempted: [] };\\n\\n  for (const mod of manifest) {\\n    report.attempted.push(mod.id);\\n    if (!mod.enabled) {\\n      report.skipped.push({ id: mod.id, reason: mod.reason ?? \\\"disabled\\\" });\\n      continue;\\n    }\\n    try {\\n      hooks.onModuleRegisterStart?.(mod);\\n      await mod.register();\\n      hooks.onModuleRegisterEnd?.(mod);\\n      report.loaded.push(mod.id);\\n    } catch (err) {\\n      hooks.onModuleRegisterEnd?.(mod);\\n      report.skipped.push({ id: mod.id, reason: `registration failed: ${String(err).slice(0, 200)}` });\\n      deps.server.log.error({ event: \\\"route-module:failed\\\", module: mod.id, error: String(err) });\\n    }\\n  }\\n\\n  return report;\\n}\\n\\n/**\\n * Snapshot of route groups (without registering anything). Useful for /_routes\\n * to surface \\\"what would have been loaded\\\" alongside the actual report.\\n */\\nexport function describeRouteManifest(deps: RouteRegistryDeps): Array<{ id: string; group: string; enabled: boolean; prefixes: string[]; family?: string; reason?: string }> {\\n  return buildRouteManifest(deps).map((m) => ({\\n    id: m.id,\\n    group: m.group ?? \\\"ungrouped\\\",\\n    enabled: m.enabled,\\n    prefixes: [...m.ownership.prefixes],\\n    ...(m.ownership.family ? { family: m.ownership.family } : {}),\\n    ...(m.reason ? { reason: m.reason } : {}),\\n  }));\\n}\\n\",\"lineCount\":308},{\"path\":\"apps/api/src/config/advisor-policy.ts\",\"content\":\"/**\\n * Advisory Model Escalation — Policy Config\\n * Object.values(PEER_MACHINES);\\n}\\n\",\"lineCount\":34},{\"path\":\"apps/api/src/config/personality-judge.ts\",\"content\":\"/**\\n * Personality Judge — Cost Controls\\n * ====================================\\n * Hard limits. Never bypassed. Never configurable at runtime.\\n */\\n\\nexport const JUDGE_LIMITS = {\\n  /** Hard ceiling on judge calls per day */\\n  daily_judge_calls_max: 100,\\n  /** Warn when daily judge cost exceeds this */\\n  daily_cost_warning_threshold: 2.00,\\n  /** Block ALL judge calls when daily cost exceeds this */\\n  daily_cost_hard_limit: 5.00,\\n  /** Max models in a single personality benchmark run */\\n  max_models_per_run: 5,\\n  /** Truncate model responses to this many tokens before sending to judge */\\n  response_truncation_tokens: 300,\\n  /** Max tokens for judge response (JSON only) */\\n  judge_max_tokens: 150,\\n} as const;\\n\\n/** Judge model options — default is Budget (Sonnet) */\\nexport const JUDGE_MODELS = {\\n  budget: {\\n    id: \\\"claude-sonnet-4-6\\\",\\n    provider: \\\"squidley.integration.provider-anthropic\\\",\\n    label: \\\"Claude Sonnet 4.6\\\",\\n    cost_per_m_input: 3.00,\\n    cost_per_m_output: 15.00,\\n  },\\n  standard: {\\n    id: \\\"claude-opus-4-6\\\",\\n    provider: \\\"squidley.integration.provider-anthropic\\\",\\n    label: \\\"Claude Opus 4.6\\\",\\n    cost_per_m_input: 15.00,\\n    cost_per_m_output: 75.00,\\n  },\\n} as const;\\n\\nexport type JudgeTier = keyof typeof JUDGE_MODELS;\\n\\n/** Estimate cost for a personality benchmark run */\\nexport function estimatePersonalityCost(\\n  modelCount: number,\\n  testCount: number,\\n  judgeTier: JudgeTier,\\n): { modelCost: number; judgeCost: number; totalCost: number; judgeCallCount: number } {\\n  const judgeModel = JUDGE_MODELS[judgeTier];\\n  const judgeCallCount = modelCount * testCount;\\n  // ~600 input tokens + ~80 output tokens per judge call\\n  const judgeCost = judgeCallCount * ((600 / 1_000_000) * judgeModel.cost_per_m_input + (80 / 1_000_000) * judgeModel.cost_per_m_output);\\n  // ~200 input + ~100 output tokens per model call (personality prompts are short)\\n  const modelCost = judgeCallCount * ((200 / 1_000_000) * 0.50 + (100 / 1_000_000) * 1.50); // estimate with cheap model\\n  return {\\n    modelCost: Math.round(modelCost * 10000) / 10000,\\n    judgeCost: Math.round(judgeCost * 10000) / 10000,\\n    totalCost: Math.round((modelCost + judgeCost) * 10000) / 10000,\\n    judgeCallCount,\\n  };\\n}\\n\\n// ── Daily tracker (in-memory, resets on restart) ────────────────────────────\\n\\nlet dailyJudgeCalls = 0;\\nlet dailyCost = 0;\\nlet lastResetDate = new Date().toISOString().slice(0, 10);\\n\\nfunction resetIfNewDay() {\\n  const today = new Date().toISOString().slice(0, 10);\\n  if (today !== lastResetDate) {\\n    dailyJudgeCalls = 0;\\n    dailyCost = 0;\\n    lastResetDate = today;\\n  }\\n}\\n\\nexport function canRunJudge(): { allowed: boolean; reason?: string } {\\n  resetIfNewDay();\\n  if (dailyJudgeCalls >= JUDGE_LIMITS.daily_judge_calls_max) {\\n    return { allowed: false, reason: `Daily judge call limit reached (${dailyJudgeCalls}/${JUDGE_LIMITS.daily_judge_calls_max})` };\\n  }\\n  if (dailyCost >= JUDGE_LIMITS.daily_cost_hard_limit) {\\n    return { allowed: false, reason: `Daily judge cost limit reached ($${dailyCost.toFixed(2)}/$${JUDGE_LIMITS.daily_cost_hard_limit.toFixed(2)})` };\\n  }\\n  return { allowed: true };\\n}\\n\\nexport function recordJudgeCall(cost: number) {\\n  resetIfNewDay();\\n  dailyJudgeCalls++;\\n  dailyCost += cost;\\n}\\n\\nexport function getJudgeUsage(): { calls: number; cost: number; limit: number; costLimit: number } {\\n  resetIfNewDay();\\n  return { calls: dailyJudgeCalls, cost: dailyCost, limit: JUDGE_LIMITS.daily_judge_calls_max, costLimit: JUDGE_LIMITS.daily_cost_hard_limit };\\n}\\n\",\"lineCount\":98},{\"path\":\"apps/api/src/config/prompt-versions.ts\",\"content\":\"/**\\n * Prompt Version Pinning\\n * =======================\\n * Tracks which version of each critical system prompt is active.\\n * Bump manually when prompts change. This matches the prior behavior in server.ts.\\n */\\nexport async function registerAllRoutes(deps: RouteRegistryDeps, hooks: RouteRegistrationHooks = {}): Promise<RouteRegistrationReport> {\\n  const manifest = buildRouteManifest(deps);\\n  const report: RouteRegistrationReport = { loaded: [], skipped: [], attempted: [] };\\n\\n  for (const mod of manifest) {\\n    report.attempted.push(mod.id);\\n    if (!mod.enabled) {\\n      report.skipped.push({ id: mod.id, reason: mod.reason ?? \\\"disabled\\\" });\\n      continue;\\n    }\\n    try {\\n      hooks.onModuleRegisterStart?.(mod);\\n      await mod.register();\\n      hooks.onModuleRegisterEnd?.(mod);\\n      report.loaded.push(mod.id);\\n    } catch (err) {\\n      hooks.onModuleRegisterEnd?.(mod);\\n      report.skipped.push({ id: mod.id, reason: `registration failed: ${String(err).slice(0, 200)}` });\\n      deps.server.log.error({ event: \\\"route-module:failed\\\", module: mod.id, error: String(err) });\\n    }\\n  }\\n\\n  return report;\\n}\\n\\n/**\\n * Snapshot of route groups (without registering anything). Useful for /_routes\\n * to surface \\\"what would have been loaded\\\" alongside the actual report.\\n */\\nexport function describeRouteManifest(deps: RouteRegistryDeps): Array<{ id: string; group: string; enabled: boolean; prefixes: string[]; family?: string; reason?: string }> {\\n  return buildRouteManifest(deps).map((m) => ({\\n    id: m.id,\\n    group: m.group ?? \\\"ungrouped\\\",\\n    enabled: m.enabled,\\n    prefixes: [...m.ownership.prefixes],\\n    ...(m.ownership.family ? { family: m.ownership.family } : {}),\\n    ...(m.reason ? { reason: m.reason } : {}),\\n  }));\\n}\\n\",\"content\":\"/**\\n * Squidley V2 — Route Registry\\n * ============================================================\\n * Single source of truth for every Fastify route module the API exposes.\\n *\\n * Why this file exists:\\n *   - Before this file landed, route registration was split between server.ts\\n *     (the documented manifest) and chat.ts (13 sub-mounted route modules\\n *     hidden inside `registerChatRoutes`). That made the runtime route\\n *     surface impossible to audit without grepping.\\n *   - Now: every public route module is declared here. server.ts wires it in.\\n *   - The `/_routes` endpoint returns the cheap route-debug inventory.\\n *   - `/runtime-truth` is the canonical live operator truth surface.\\n *\\n * Adding a new route module:\\n *   1. import its `registerXyzRoutes` function\\n *   2. add an entry to `buildRouteManifest()`\\n *   3. that's it — boot will pick it up, /_routes will surface route-debug\\n *      inventory and /runtime-truth will surface canonical Pure function of deps — no side effects until\\n * `registerAllRoutes` walks it.\\n *\\n * Sub-mounts that used to live inside `registerChatRoutes` are now declared\\n * here. They run BEFORE chat itself so any service/event subscriptions they\\n * register are in place when /chat is hit (preserving the previous boot order\\n * within chat.ts).\\n */\\nexport function buildRouteManifest(deps: RouteRegistryDeps): RouteModule[] {\\n  const { server, getService, platformCtx, registry, forax, cursus, legatus, stealth } = deps;\\n  const s = server as any; // some registrars have stricter Fastify typings\\n\\n  return [\\n    // ── Chat-surface sub-mounts (lifted out of chat.ts) ────────────────────\\n    // These run first so their decorators / hooks / shared state are ready\\n    // before /chat begins serving. Boot order is preserved from the old\\n    // sequence in chat.ts (codex → cc → tenticode → … → imperium).\\n    { id: \\\"codex\\\",                     group: \\\"chat-surface\\\", enabled: true, ownership: { prefixes: [\\\"/forge\\\", \\\"/codex\\\"], family: \\\"chat_surface\\\" }, register: () => registerCodexRoutes(s, getService) },\\n    { id: \\\"cc\\\",                        group: This matches the prior behavior in server.ts.\\n */\\nexport async function registerAllRoutes(deps: RouteRegistryDeps, hooks: RouteRegistrationHooks = {}): Promise<RouteRegistrationReport> {\\n  const manifest = buildRouteManifest(deps);\\n  const report: RouteRegistrationReport = { loaded: [], skipped: [], attempted: [] };\\n\\n  for (const mod of manifest) {\\n    report.attempted.push(mod.id);\\n    if (!mod.enabled) {\\n      report.skipped.push({ id: mod.id, reason: mod.reason ?? \\\"disabled\\\" });\\n      continue;\\n    }\\n    try {\\n      hooks.onModuleRegisterStart?.(mod);\\n      await mod.register();\\n      hooks.onModuleRegisterEnd?.(mod);\\n      report.loaded.push(mod.id);\\n    } catch (err) {\\n      hooks.onModuleRegisterEnd?.(mod);\\n      report.skipped.push({ id: mod.id, reason: `registration failed: ${String(err).slice(0, 200)}` });\\n      deps.server.log.error({ event: \\\"route-module:failed\\\", module: mod.id, error: String(err) });\\n    }\\n  }\\n\\n  return report;\\n}\\n\\n/**\\n * Snapshot of route groups (without registering anything).
